last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler enable-flow slice 5 — pg_authid SCRAM verifier query. internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err) reads pg_authid.rolpassword for the named app roles — the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed. rolpassword is superuser-only so it ALWAYS uses the privileged psql path (runPsql, postgres OS user, peer auth), never the non-superuser read pool. Secret-adjacent: logs nothing (runPsql logs only argv/role-names, never stdout/verifiers); query has no PASSWORD literal so Sensitive stays false. Strict (auth_file boundary): each name ValidateIdentifier-gated AND QuoteLiteral'd into the IN-list (defense in depth); missing role -> NotFound naming it; NULL/empty rolpassword -> Validation error (never silently dropped — a missing entry would lock that app out); duplicates de-duped; deterministic sort by name. SCRAM-vs-md5 NOT checked here — RenderUserlist is the single gate (verbatim pass-through proven). Remaining for enable flow: apt package install + service lifecycle (systemctl enable --now + SIGHUP reload/restart-fallback only when changed + verify running), then UI toggle.
_prev_last_item: Band-3 (usability): PgBouncer pooler slice 4b — atomic auth_file (userlist.txt) installer. internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureUserlist renders via RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically (temp+rename, 0640 owner pgbouncer, O_NOFOLLOW guard, deterministic no-op, NO marker guard — ownership gated upstream by EnsureConfig's .ini marker). DRYed shared atomicInstall(path,data,mode). PRIOR slice 4a: PgBouncer pooler slice 4a — SCRAM userlist render. internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) builds the auth_file (userlist.txt) text — `"username" "verifier"` lines. Pure render, no file I/O. Security-strict: >=1 entry required (empty auth_file locks all apps out); every verifier must be SCRAM-SHA-256$ + verifier-alphabet only (md5/plaintext refused, never downgraded; charset doubles as injection guard); usernames with quote/whitespace/control/line-separator rejected (not escaped); duplicate usernames refused; deterministic sort so unchanged set is byte-identical (skip reload). Carved out of the enable-flow item as its own slice. Remaining for the enable flow: query pg_authid for verifiers, atomic install of userlist.txt, pkg install + service lifecycle.
last_result: done (iter 38) — PgBouncer pooler ENABLE-FLOW SLICE 5: the pg_authid SCRAM verifier query, the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed. Added internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err): reads pg_authid.rolpassword for the named app roles. KEY: rolpassword is superuser-only, so it ALWAYS goes through the privileged psql path (runPsql, AsUser postgres, peer auth over the local socket) — never the panel's non-superuser read pool (mirrors settings.go showSetting's superuser-GUC fallback). SECRET-ADJACENT: the function logs nothing; runPsql logs only argv (the SELECT + role names — not secret), never stdout (the verifiers); the query carries no PASSWORD literal so Sensitive stays correctly false. STRICT (the auth_file is a boundary): every requested name is core.ValidateIdentifier-gated AND core.QuoteLiteral'd into the IN-list (defense in depth — the name still travels through SQL); a requested role absent from pg_authid is a NotFound naming it (can't pool a role that isn't there); a role with NULL/empty rolpassword is a Validation error, never silently dropped (a missing auth_file entry would lock that app out of the pooler); duplicate requests de-duped; output sorted by name (deterministic -> byte-identical auth_file -> enable flow can skip a reload). SCRAM-vs-md5/plaintext is DELIBERATELY not checked here — pgbouncer.RenderUserlist is the single gate that refuses a non-SCRAM verifier (no downgrade); RoleVerifiers returns the stored verifier VERBATIM (proven: an md5 verifier is returned here, refused downstream). Tests verifiers_test.go: superuser-path (AsUser postgres + pg_authid + quoted literals in argv), verbatim non-SCRAM pass-through, request de-dup (one literal in IN-list), missing-role NotFound, empty-verifier Validation, invalid-name (quote/space/semicolon/empty) rejected BEFORE any psql runs, requires-runner, empty-request. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no critical; applied the one Important finding — dropped a dead `ORDER BY rolname` (rows are parsed into a map and re-sorted in Go via sort.Strings, so the SQL ordering was unused work). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 37) — PgBouncer pooler SLICE 4b: atomic auth_file (userlist.txt) installer, mirroring how slice 3 split the .ini's atomic write out from service lifecycle. Added Manager.EnsureUserlist(ctx, []UserlistEntry) (changed bool, err) in internal/pgbouncer/install.go: renders via the (slice-4a) RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically — temp+rename, 0640 owned by the pgbouncer user (the auth_file holds secret-adjacent SCRAM verifiers, never world-readable), O_NOFOLLOW symlink-hijack guard surfaced as CodeConflict (target untouched), deterministic no-op when content is byte-identical so the enable flow can skip a needless reload; logs only the user COUNT, never a verifier. KEY DECISION: NO foreign-file marker guard (unlike the .ini) — the userlist.txt format is pure `"user" "verifier"` lines and cannot carry an in-file marker (confirmed against the sm source: services/pgbouncer.py writes pure entries). Ownership is gated UPSTREAM by EnsureConfig's marker guard on pgbouncer.ini — the enable flow only reaches the auth_file once indiepg owns the .ini, the auth_file being a fully indiepg-derived satellite of that managed config; RenderUserlist's SCRAM-only/injection validation still hard-stops any bad entry before a write. DRYed the shared atomic-install logic out of writeConfigFile into a package-level atomicInstall(path, data, mode) used by both the .ini and the auth_file (EnsureConfig behavior unchanged — pure mechanical extraction). Tests userlist_install_test.go: writes-0640 (exact RenderUserlist bytes), idempotent-no-rewrite (mtime-stable; a two-user set re-submitted in REVERSE input order proves the sort-stable no-op at the install level), rewrites-on-change, refuses-symlink, rejects-non-SCRAM + rejects-empty before any write, requires-runner, default-path. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking findings — applied the one Important test fix (idempotency test now actually reorders a two-user set instead of re-submitting a single entry). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 36) — PgBouncer pooler SLICE 4a: pure SCRAM userlist render, carving out the substantive sub-piece the backlog flagged. Added internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go: RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) (string, error) builds the auth_file (userlist.txt) text as `"username" "verifier"` lines (sm/pgbouncer format), pure render (no file I/O — atomic install+chown lands in the enable slice like the .ini installer). SECURITY-STRICT (auth_file is a boundary): at least one entry required (empty auth_file silently locks every app out of the pooler); EVERY verifier must start SCRAM-SHA-256$ and contain only the verifier alphabet [A-Za-z0-9+/=:$-] — md5/plaintext REFUSED, never downgraded, and the charset check doubles as the injection guard for the quoted token; usernames with a quote/whitespace/control char/Unicode line separator rejected (not escaped) so a crafted name can't inject a second auth entry; duplicate usernames refused (pgbouncer silently honours only the first). DETERMINISTIC (sorted by username) so an unchanged user set renders byte-identical and the future enable flow can skip a needless reload. Mirrors the pure-render pattern of config.go/pool.go. Tests userlist_test.go: line-format, sorted-stable/order-independent, empty-refused, non-SCRAM-refused (md5/plaintext/wrong-case/wrong-algo), verifier-injection (quote/newline/space/#), username-injection (quote/space/tab/newline/U+2028/U+2029/NUL/empty), duplicate-refused, no-write-on-error. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): two test-hardening findings applied — explicit U+2028/U+2029 separator cases (was an opaque raw byte) + a CodeValidation assertion on the empty-slice path (the production code had no bugs/bypasses). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 35) — PgBouncer pooler SLICE 3: config installer, mirroring how the pgBackRest installer split the atomic config write out from stanza-create. Added internal/pgbouncer/install.go: Manager (Runner/Logger/ConfDir; ConfDir defaults /etc/pgbouncer) with EnsureConfig(ConfigParams) (changed bool, err) — renders slice-2's RenderConfig and installs the pgbouncer.ini ATOMICALLY (temp+rename), owner pgbouncer 0640 (DEFAULTS.md; .ini holds no secret, SCRAM verifiers live in the separate auth_file), returns whether it changed. SAFETY (mirrors backup/provision.go): marker-guarded — a config lacking indiepg's first-line marker (hand-written/distro pgbouncer.ini) is surfaced as CodeConflict, never clobbered; deterministic render -> byte-compare no-op when unchanged so the future enable flow can skip a needless reload; O_NOFOLLOW path-hijack guard refuses a symlink at the config path as a clear CodeConflict; chown to pgbouncer user root-fatal/non-root-best-effort. NO package-install/service-touch yet (apt + SCRAM userlist + systemctl enable --now + SIGHUP reload/restart-fallback + verify running -> next slice). Tests install_test.go: writes-marked-0640, idempotent-no-rewrite (mtime-stable), rewrites-on-change, refuses-foreign + marker-not-first-line, refuses-symlink (target untouched), rejects-injection-before-any-write, requires-runner, default-confdir. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): applied the 1 actionable finding — symlink case now returns clear CodeConflict not opaque CodeInternal (honors the 'errors loudly' contract); kept the runner nil-guard (consistent Manager invariant, used next slice). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 34) — PgBouncer pooler SLICE 2: pure pgbouncer.ini render (no install yet), mirroring how RecommendTuning was built (pure render before any host write). Added internal/pgbouncer/config.go: RenderConfig(ConfigParams) renders the full ini from a PoolRecommendation (slice 1) + host bits (pg/listen ports, auth_file, pidfile, admin user), every field safe-defaulted so a zero ConfigParams (only Pool set) still renders valid. SECURITY PINS (non-configurable): listen_addr + [databases] upstream host both hardcoded 127.0.0.1 (loopback-only, never widened); [databases] * = host=127.0.0.1 port=<pg>; hardened constants auth_type scram-sha-256 / pool_mode transaction / server_reset_query DISCARD ALL straight from pool.go. Deterministic (fixed line order) so an unchanged config renders byte-identical and the installer can skip rewrite+reload. Injection-hardened: every interpolated value validated against control chars + U+2028/29 line separators + '#'/';' comment starters (an embedded ';' would silently truncate a value to a comment); bad/colliding ports rejected; zero/negative pool refused (0 server_idle_timeout disables idle reclamation -> server slots leak forever). ConfigMarker first-line ownership guard (mirrors pgBackRest) + HasManagedMarker so the install slice never clobbers an operator/distro file. NO install/file-write yet (atomic write owner pgbouncer 0640 -> next slice). Tests config_test.go: default structure + hardened settings, custom params interpolated, never-widens/weakens (no 0.0.0.0/::/trust/any/plain), determinism, injection (newline/CR/U+2028/;/#), port validation+collision, zero/negative pool rejection, marker recognition incl mid-file false-positive. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): applied the 1 critical (validate Pool numbers) + all 3 important (reject #/;, add :: to never-widen test, zero-pool rejection test). Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 33) — Two band-3 items remained. DROPPED (a) 'provision flow shows computed best-defaults': provisioning is CLI-driven (indiepg install), App.tsx has no provision route, so no surface; intent already served read-only by the iter-31 Database-tuning card. Then started the largest remaining item — the OPT-IN PgBouncer pooler — with its smallest atomic slice (mirroring RecommendTuning's pure-math-first build): internal/pgbouncer/pool.go. RecommendPool(pgMaxConnections, profile) ports the sm pool math (verified vs server-management/.../pgbouncer.py + DEFAULTS.md): available = max_conn-5 reserved; default_pool_size = int(available*util) floored 20 (util oltp .80/mixed .70/olap .60); min = default/4 floor 5; reserve = default/5 floor 5; max_client_conn = default*multiplex (20/10/5); idle 300/600. Pure/total: reuses pg.WorkloadProfile (unknown->Mixed), clamps max_conn<1, panic-free. Fixed safe defaults hardcoded constants (auth_type scram-sha-256, pool_mode transaction, server_reset_query DISCARD ALL — never weakened), surfaced via SettingsMap(). Tests: hand-computed table (mixed/oltp/olap + floor box), invariants across small->large boxes (floors fire, sub-pools >=5, max_client = default*multiplex, pool never exceeds PG capacity above floor, idle per profile), degenerate/negative + unknown-profile, SettingsMap asserting hardened constants + numbers. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no critical; applied both important findings — corrected DEFAULTS.md round->int(...) truncation to match sm, widened invariant sweep ({1,5,10,20,...}) to self-cover floors. Gates: gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./..., CGO_ENABLED=0 build all green; no web/ touched. ---PRIOR done (iter 32) — audited every confirmation site (Backups run/deep-test/restore-overwrite, Alerts delete-rule, RolesDatabases drop db/user, Migrate single/cluster overwrite): each already states the consequence + irreversibility, and destructive ops gate behind a typed exact-name match. The audit was completely untested — a refactor could silently weaken the typed-name gate that stands between the operator and irreversible data loss. Added web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.test.tsx (8 tests, first coverage for these primitives): ConfirmDialog renders title/message + fires confirm/cancel, danger tone -> btn-danger, busy disables both + 'Working…' + disabled confirm is no-op, closed renders nothing; TypedConfirmDialog states what is destroyed + 'cannot be undone', surfaces the caller consequence inside .callout-danger, keeps delete DISABLED until exact name typed (near-miss stays disabled + flags input invalid; disabled click never calls onConfirm), fires onConfirm(typed) only on exact match, stays gated while busy. No component changes (pure test hardening). Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): two robustness findings — assert the danger callout by container class not the accidental text node, and assert input 'not invalid' rather than exact aria-invalid='false' serialisation — both applied. Gates: gofmt clean, go build OK (no Go touched), web typecheck + deterministic build (no dist diff) + 55 vitest tests green. ---PRIOR done (iter 31) — provisioning is CLI-driven, so the Provision result's recommended_tuning/tuning fields were only logged, never surfaced. Added a READ-ONLY surface. Backend: Manager.CurrentTuning(ctx) in internal/pg/tuning_status.go returns detected RAM/CPU, the live applied settings (read via the existing readTunableSettings from pg_settings, normalised to whole MB), and the host-sized recommendation per profile (oltp/mixed/olap via pure RecommendTuning through the hostTuning seam). BEST-EFFORT: if Postgres is unreachable it returns Applied=nil with NO error so the recommendations still load. handleGetTuning wired at GET /api/tuning (read-only; never mutates PG). Frontend: 'Database tuning (host-sized)' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows the box's RAM/CPU + active profile, the applied settings (each plainly described), and a profile selector that PREVIEWS what each profile would size the box to, every profile labeled by effect; a non-default selection clearly says it's a preview that changes nothing and that a real switch needs a brief Postgres restart (so it's an install/provision-time action, not a panel button — security tie-break: NO restart-trigger surface in the panel). Tests: TestCurrentTuning_SurfacesAppliedAndAllProfiles + TestCurrentTuning_DegradesWhenPostgresUnreachable; DatabaseTuning.test.tsx (mbLabel GB/MB units, host+active render, applied values, null-applied calm warn with recommendations still shown, profile preview describes effect + asserts the no-change copy). Full Go gate (gofmt/vet/test/build) + web gate (typecheck/build/47 vitest tests) green. Reviewed (feature-dev:code-reviewer): no blocking issues; tidied one redundant hostTuning(Mixed) detection call to reuse profiles[1]. BAND 2.5 COMPLETE.
notes: BANDS 1.5 + 2 + 2.5 COMPLETE; BAND 3 (usability) IN PROGRESS. Provision-flow item (a) DROPPED iter 33 (no panel provision route — CLI install; served by the tuning card). The ONLY remaining band-3 feature is the OPT-IN PgBouncer pooler, now being built incrementally like RecommendTuning was. PgBouncer slice 1 DONE iter 33: pure host-sized pool math internal/pgbouncer/pool.go (RecommendPool + SettingsMap, ports sm/DEFAULTS.md: avail=max_conn-5, default_pool=int(avail*util) floor20, min=/4 floor5, reserve=/5 floor5, max_client=default*multiplex, idle 300/600; safe defaults scram/transaction/DISCARD-ALL hardcoded). NEXT band-3 sub-items (in order): (i) DONE iter 34 — pgbouncer.ini render internal/pgbouncer/config.go RenderConfig; (ii) DONE iter 35 — config installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureConfig (atomic temp+rename, owner pgbouncer 0640, marker-guarded foreign->CodeConflict, deterministic no-op, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard); (iii-a) DONE iter 36 — SCRAM userlist render internal/pgbouncer/userlist.go RenderUserlist([]UserlistEntry) (pure render of the `"user" "verifier"` auth_file: >=1 entry, SCRAM-only/no-downgrade, verifier+username injection guards, dup-refused, deterministic sort); (iii-b) DONE iter 37 — atomic auth_file installer internal/pgbouncer/install.go Manager.EnsureUserlist(ctx, []UserlistEntry) (renders via RenderUserlist then installs userlist.txt atomically: temp+rename, 0640 owner pgbouncer, O_NOFOLLOW symlink guard, deterministic no-op; NO marker guard since the format can't carry one — ownership gated upstream by EnsureConfig's .ini marker, auth_file is a derived satellite; logs only user count; DRYed shared atomicInstall(path,data,mode) out of writeConfigFile); (iii-c) DONE iter 38 — pg_authid SCRAM verifier query internal/pg/verifiers.go Manager.RoleVerifiers(ctx, roleNames []string) ([]RoleVerifier, err): the one input EnsureUserlist/RenderUserlist still needed fed, read via the PRIVILEGED psql path (rolpassword is superuser-only) — logs nothing, ValidateIdentifier+QuoteLiteral names, missing-role NotFound, empty-rolpassword Validation (never dropped), de-dup, deterministic sort, verbatim pass-through (SCRAM gate stays in RenderUserlist); (iii-d) <== NEXT enable flow backend REMAINDER (opt-in, OFF by default): apt install pgbouncer pkg + orchestrate RoleVerifiers -> EnsureUserlist + EnsureConfig + systemctl enable --now + SIGHUP reload/restart-fallback ONLY when config/userlist changed + verify running; idempotent; any restart follows sm safety conventions; (iv) UI toggle (off by default) showing the app connection string via the pooler + pool settings labeled by effect, with a confirm stating exactly what enabling does + vitest test. After these, band 3 is complete -> band 4 (UI redo, shadcn). DONE iter 32: destructive-confirm AUDIT + TESTS — all confirm sites already state consequence + irreversibility and gate destructive ops behind a typed exact-name match; was untested, so added web/src/components/ConfirmDialog.test.tsx (8 tests) locking: plain-language consequence shown, TypedConfirmDialog cannot fire onConfirm until exact name typed (also gated while busy). No component changes. Band-3 item (b) DONE. Test-only tip: web npm/npx from repo ROOT hits a read-only ~/.npm cache (EROFS) when it tries to fetch — run the web gate from web/ (npm run typecheck/build/test) or invoke web/node_modules/.bin/{vitest,tsc} directly with the project path. NEXT IS BAND 3 (usability): top open items — (a) provision flow shows computed best-defaults up front with each override labeled by effect, safe to accept blindly; (c) PgBouncer opt-in pooler toggle (DEFAULTS.md pool math, off by default, shows app connection string). NOTE the tuning UI just shipped is read-only/preview; if a real in-panel profile-switch is ever wanted it MUST funnel through ApplyTuning's restartWithRollback + a confirm dialog stating the brief restart (fits band-3 destructive-confirm item) — but it is NOT load-bearing for the north star, so only add it if usability work surfaces a genuine need. DONE iter 31: APPLY host-sized tuning UI SURFACE — read-only GET /api/tuning (Manager.CurrentTuning, internal/pg/tuning_status.go) returns detected RAM/CPU + live applied settings (from pg_settings, whole MB, Applied=nil best-effort when PG unreachable) + per-profile host-sized recommendation; 'Database tuning' card on Settings (web/src/views/DatabaseTuning.tsx) shows applied settings + a per-profile PREVIEW labeled by effect, with copy making clear a non-default selection changes nothing and a real switch needs a restart (no restart-trigger surface). DONE iter 30: APPLY host-sized tuning REAL-PG INTEGRATION TEST (internal/pg/tuning_apply_integration_test.go, //go:build integration, INDIEPG_PG_BINDIR-gated). Proves ApplyTuning end-to-end on a throwaway PG14 cluster: settings land in pg_settings (bytes via unit col, max_connections exact), 2nd run no-op, an unstartable max_connections=1 rolls back via restartWithRollback with PG up -> CodeSafety. tuningTestRunner translates the exact `systemctl restart postgresql` -> pg_ctl restart. INTEGRATION-TEST GOTCHAS: force a deterministic boot failure with a CROSS-GUC constraint (max_connections=1), NOT an oversized memory GUC (overcommit makes it hang); and pg_ctl start/restart needs -l <logfile> or the daemonized postmaster holds the captured stdout pipe and cmd.Run hangs forever. ONLY REMAINING band-2.5 item: the ApplyTuning UI SURFACE — show the applied host-sized defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect (oltp/olap/mixed); vitest/RTL test; off-the-default profile clearly described before it changes sizing. After that, band 2.5 is complete -> band 3 (usability). DONE iter 29: APPLY host-sized tuning at provision — Manager.ApplyTuning (internal/pg/tuning_apply.go) persists RecommendTuning's five settings via ALTER SYSTEM, idempotent vs pg_settings (bytes-normalised through the unit col), restart-requiring (shared_buffers/max_connections) funneled through restartWithRollback, reloadable via pg_reload_conf. Wired into Provision (replaces iter-28 'surfaced, not applied'); a CodeSafety rollback is tolerated gracefully (provision still succeeds on prior good config). detectTuning seam added for deterministic provision tests. Fully unit-tested. THE TWO NEW TOP BAND-2.5 ITEMS are the apply follow-ups: (a) a real-PG //go:build integration test proving settings land in pg_settings, a 2nd run no-ops, and an oversized shared_buffers rolls back with PG up; (b) the UI surface showing the applied defaults (Provision already returns recommended_tuning + tuning status) + the workload-profile override labeled by effect. ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — ApplyTuning is the worked example. DONE iter 28: host-sized tuning PURE SIZING FUNCTION — RecommendTuning(memoryMB, cpuCount, profile) in internal/pg/tuning.go ports the sm tuning.py math (DEFAULTS.md) for shared_buffers/effective_cache_size/work_mem/maintenance_work_mem/max_connections; WorkloadProfile selectable, empty->Mixed default, unknown->ValidationError; total/panic-free on degenerate input. Tested vs sample RAM 1GB-128GB x all 3 profiles + clamp/ordering/degenerate/parse/SettingsMap. DONE iter 27: connection-saturation alert CRITICAL tier connections-critical (pg.connections_percent >= 95%, For 1m, Cooldown 15m, Critical) escalating the existing connections-near-max WARNING (85%); rule-only, metric already real (sampler.go pg_stat_activity / max_connections), ID-based seed auto-adds on upgrade; TestConnectionSaturationTiers proves both tiers fire non-vacuously. DONE iter 26: SELF-HEALING config primitive (internal/pg/safeconfig.go): snapshotAutoConf captures <data_directory>/postgresql.auto.conf BEFORE a restart-requiring change (fails closed if unreadable); restartWithRollback restarts PG and on non-zero `systemctl restart` exit restores the snapshot via `tee` as the postgres user (keeps owner+0600; works while PG is down, unlike ALTER SYSTEM RESET) + restarts again so PG is never left down -> CodeSafety ('rolled back; PG running') or CodeInternal ('PG down'+hint). ANY future config-apply/override flow MUST funnel its restart through restartWithRollback (snapshot before write) — this is exactly what the new APPLY item above must do. DONE iter 25: disk-headroom-low early-warning rule (WARNING 80% host.disk_percent) below critical disk-almost-full (90%). KEY: adding a disk/conn alert is usually RULE-ONLY — Snapshot already has the fields, alert/metrics.go metricValue computes the percent, and background.go's seedDefaultAlertRules inserts only missing IDs (idempotent) so a new DefaultRules() entry auto-seeds on upgrade. Test pattern: extend TestDefaultRules want-list + a focused engine test; background_test asserts len(DefaultRules()) dynamically. --- Alert plumbing: Collector.SampleOnce -> Engine.Evaluate -> dispatchAlertEvents (background.go); metricValue (alert/metrics.go) reads Snapshot fields; go is a snap -> run go/psql/pg_ctl sandbox-disabled; empty $TMPDIR in some shells -> use /tmp. --- HISTORICAL: band 1.5 (backups proven-restorable, S3 nudge, restore-test verify+deep, scheduled jobs) and band 2 (web silent-failure audit, idempotent provisioning, query-box auto-LIMIT+statement_timeout enforced) all COMPLETE. See git log.
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
